title: Angular Advanced — Paper 3
description: Advanced Angular concepts: reactive forms, custom validators, routing, guards, HTTP client, RxJS, change detection, custom directives/pipes, state management, and testing.

questions.text: Which FormBuilder method creates a FormGroup with shorthand syntax — passing an array where the first element is the value and subsequent elements are validators?

options.text: this.fb.group({ name: new FormControl('', Validators.required) })
this.fb.group({ name: ['', Validators.required] })
this.fb.control(['name', Validators.required])
this.fb.array({ name: '', validators: [Validators.required] })

questions.text: What is a nested FormGroup and why would you use one?

options.text: A FormGroup inside a FormArray — used to repeat sets of related fields
A FormGroup nested inside another FormGroup — used to logically group related controls such as an address with street, city, and zip
A FormGroup that uses lazy validation
A FormGroup created with fb.nested() for hierarchical data
FormControl
AbstractControl (which can be a FormGroup in practice)

questions.text: What is the purpose of a named router-outlet in Angular?

options.text: To restrict which components can be loaded into it
To allow multiple independent router outlets in the same template, each rendering a different route simultaneously
To give the outlet a CSS class for styling
To make the outlet lazy-loaded

questions.text: In a CanDeactivateFn guard, the guard receives the component instance. What would you typically check before allowing navigation away?

options.text: Whether the user is authenticated
Whether the component has unsaved changes by calling a method like component.hasUnsavedChanges()
Whether the target route exists
Whether the route has a resolver

questions.text: What does CanMatchFn do that is different from CanActivateFn?

options.text: CanMatchFn runs after the component is loaded; CanActivateFn runs before
CanMatchFn prevents the route from being matched at all (the router tries the next route); CanActivateFn prevents navigation but the route is still matched
CanMatchFn is used for child routes; CanActivateFn is for top-level routes
They are identical functional guards
Filters products where price > 1.2
Transforms each emitted Product by increasing its price by 20% and returns a new object
Logs the product to console
Retries the HTTP call 1.2 times

questions.text: In an HttpInterceptorFn, how do you modify a response before it reaches the component?

options.text: Intercept the request and return a modified req object
Call next(req) and pipe operators like map() on the returned observable to transform the HttpEvent
Use res.clone() after calling next()
Angular does not support response modification in interceptors
The previous request is cancelled and a new one starts
The new click is ignored and no new request is made until the current one completes
Both requests run concurrently
The requests are queued and run one after another

questions.text: Which RxJS operator would you use to listen for type-ahead search and cancel previous HTTP requests when the user types again?

options.text: concatMap
switchMap
mergeMap

questions.text: What is the difference between detach() and reattach() on ChangeDetectorRef?

options.text: detach() removes the component from the DOM; reattach() inserts it back
detach() removes the component from Angular's change detection tree so it is never checked; reattach() re-adds it so it is checked again
detach() clears the component's bindings; reattach() restores them
They are used for lazy-loading component trees

questions.text: In Angular, what are signals and how do they differ from traditional observables for state management?

options.text: Signals are components; observables are services

Signals are synchronous reactive primitives that hold a value and notify dependents when it changes without needing subscriptions; observables are push-based streams that require subscription management
Signals are only for HTTP responses; observables are for UI events
Signals replace NgRx; observables are used only in effects
Attribute directive that changes the element's style
Structural-like directive that controls whether a view is rendered based on permissions
Component directive that injects a template
Pipe that transforms permission strings

questions.text: What does select: 'header' on ng-content do?

options.text: Projects only header elements from CSS
Projects only child content matching the CSS selector 'header' into that specific ng-content slot
Creates a named slot called 'header'
Selects a template from the parent component
It caches the store reference permanently
It memoizes the result and only recomputes when the input state slice changes, preventing unnecessary recalculation
It parallelizes state computations
It prevents components from subscribing to the store

questions.text: What does toObservable() do in Angular Signals?

options.text: Converts an observable to a signal
Converts a signal into an observable so it can be used with RxJS operators and async pipe
Creates a computed observable from multiple signals
Schedules signal updates as asynchronous events
To reset the component
To trigger Angular's change detection so the template reflects the updated component property before the DOM is queried
To compile the template
To initialize the TestBed

questions.text: What is the purpose of fakeAsync() in Angular testing?

options.text: It replaces real HTTP calls with mock responses
It wraps the test in a zone that allows controlling asynchronous operations (setTimeout, Promises) synchronously with tick()
It creates a fake component fixture
It disables change detection during the test

questions.text: Which lazy loading strategy delays loading until the user navigates close to a lazy-loaded route, rather than loading everything upfront or on demand?

options.text: PreloadAllModules
QuicklinkStrategy (from ngx-quicklink)
NoPreloading
OnDemandStrategy

questions.text: In RxJS, what does the Subject class allow that a regular Observable does not?

options.text: Subscribing to multiple sources simultaneously
Being both an Observable (can be subscribed to) and an Observer (can emit values by calling next/error/complete)
Automatically completing after one emission
Replaying the last N values to new subscribers

questions.text: In Angular forms, what is the updateOn option in FormControl and what does it control?

options.text: When the control renders in the DOM
The event that triggers value and validity updates — can be 'change' (default), 'blur', or 'submit'
Whether the control value is emitted to the parent form
The animation trigger for validation messages

questions.text: In Angular security, what is the risk of using [innerHTML]="userContent" and how does Angular mitigate it?

options.text: innerHTML can expose CSRF tokens; Angular encrypts them
innerHTML can inject malicious scripts (XSS); Angular sanitizes the HTML before binding, stripping dangerous tags and attributes
innerHTML causes memory leaks; Angular runs a garbage collector
innerHTML overrides styles; Angular re-applies view encapsulation

questions.text: What does detectChanges() on ChangeDetectorRef do compared to ApplicationRef.tick()?

options.text: They are identical
detectChanges() runs change detection only for the specific component and its children; ApplicationRef.tick() runs it for the entire application tree
detectChanges() is used for OnPush; ApplicationRef.tick() is for Default strategy
detectChanges() is synchronous; ApplicationRef.tick() is always asynchronous
switchMap is required by NgRx effects
switchMap cancels any in-flight request if loadUsers is dispatched again, preventing stale responses from overwriting newer ones
mergeMap cannot be used with HTTP observables
switchMap provides automatic retry logic

questions.text: What does the Angular @Pipe decorator's standalone: true option enable?

options.text: The pipe can be used without declaring it in any module or component
The pipe can be imported directly into standalone components or other modules without being declared in a shared NgModule
The pipe runs outside the Angular zone
The pipe is compiled ahead-of-time only

questions.text: In HTTP interceptors, how do you pass metadata to an interceptor from a specific HTTP call?

options.text: Using HttpHeaders with a custom header
Using HttpContext with a HttpContextToken to attach arbitrary data to the request
Adding a custom query parameter
Using the interceptor's constructor injection

questions.text: What is the purpose of Validators.compose() in Angular reactive forms?

options.text: Combines multiple validators and runs them in parallel, returning all errors
Combines an array of validator functions into a single validator that runs them in order and merges all validation errors
Creates an async validator from sync validators
Composes a form group from a list of controls
